prompt: Use the provided image as the base reference for the original character. Produce a high-quality anime key visual featuring both the original character and their gender-swapped version simultaneously. Place the original character on the left side of the screen. Place the gender-swapped character on the right side of the screen. Both must be immediately recognizable as the same person at a glance. Maintain facial features, eye shape, facial proportions, hairstyle design language, hair color, silhouette, habitual expressions, personality traits, visual motifs, and overall character identity with high precision. The gender-swapped version should not be mere cosplay or an outfit swap, but a design based on the natural interpretation of "what if the same person was born as the opposite gender from the start." Do not simply put them in clothes for the opposite sex. While naturally redesigning the outfit to suit the gender, maintain the fashion style, color design, accessories, symbolism, occupation, social status, world-view, and design philosophy of the original design. Ensure the audience can instantly understand that they are "male and female versions of the same person." Both designs should share strong visual commonalities. [Overall Atmosphere] The vibe of a late 1990s to early 2000s Japanese anime magazine or promotional poster. An official key visual for a non-existent masterpiece gender-swapped rom-com anime. Joyful confusion. Lighthearted commotion. Rom-com-style distance. Comical pacing. Not a serious sci-fi work. Not a serious parallel world story. Not a global crisis. Prioritize a fun atmosphere as if some unexpected trouble has occurred. Aim for a promotional visual that makes viewers think, "This definitely looks like it would be fun." [Character Acting] Depict the two characters as having accidentally encountered their own opposite-gender self. They should be aware of and reacting to each other. Emotions can be chosen freely. Examples: Surprise, confusion, bewilderment, irritation, curiosity, embarrassment, rivalry, self-loathing, self-praise, exasperation, provocation, distrust, caution, strange sense of affinity, etc. A mix of multiple emotions is allowed. The important part is to convey the humor of "seeing one's own gender-swapped self." [Composition] Do not use a fixed composition. Choose a different staging every time. Examples: Facing each other, back-to-back, putting arms around shoulders, arguing, glaring at each other, pointing at each other, staring at each other like looking in a mirror, taking the same pose, taking completely different poses, observing each other, chasing each other, running/chasing, accidentally bumping into each other, confusion immediately after swapping, fighting over the same object, reacting simultaneously, checking each other's clothing, keeping distance warily, becoming awkward from being too close, etc. Do not rely on symmetrical compositions. Vary the composition so that it looks like a promotional visual for a different anime work every time. [Background] The background should naturally suit the character's world-view, occupation, era setting, genre, culture, and design language. Design the background as a high-quality anime backdrop that enhances the characters. If necessary, you may use: School, cityscape, shopping district, fantasy world, Japanese-style space, magical world, cyberpunk city, retro-pop design, amusement park, school festival, stage, abstract graphics, etc. The background should naturally convey the genre of the work. [Art Style] Late 1990s to early 2000s anime magazine illustrations. Retro-pop anime aesthetics. Cel-shaded animation style. Clean line art. Bold silhouette design. Vivid and joyful color design. Commercial anime official promotional illustration quality. Dynamic like a scene from an anime opening. Strong eye-catch like a magazine cover. [Prohibitions] No logos. No title text. No captions. No watermarks. No comparison table layouts. No setting material collection layouts. No turnaround sheets. Do not make it a character comparison image. Do not make it a serious multiverse war. Do not make it a dark fantasy apocalyptic battle. Do not let combat or world-view explanations take center stage over the gender swap itself.
